aliases: how other users see my topics
blurb: Drill in when an approved topic shows on one machine but not a teammate's, or when wiring git-based topic sync across clones.
intent: Explain how regin's standalone-per-user model lets one user's approved topics become visible to other users: what travels via git (topic.json + wiki/<id>.md), what stays local (topic.local.json overlay, snapshot DB, in-flight proposals), and the producer/consumer steps (promote -> pre-commit add -> teammate git pull -> post-merge `topics import` -> snapshot DB seeded -> routable & visible in WebUI). Also covers the shared-server alternative when cross-user proposal review is needed.
label: Multi-user topic sharing (how approved topics travel from one user to another)
